body_txt: In fiction, mice are more often than not portrayed as the good guys , being pitted against predators like cats and helped out by friendlier animals like dogs ... ...but, they are not without exceptions. Perhaps because of their history with humans, mice are also likely to be portrayed in fiction as troublesome antagonists, or at least as very unwelcome pests. While usually not seen as outright disgusting and evil in the way that rats are , mice are still often thought of as irritating, undesirable creatures. There's a reason, after all, why the freaked-out reaction people have to finding one in their house has become a trope of its own . Mice are also not uncommon as an animal phobia or tending to bring fear to elephants . These mice are often portrayed as pranksters, thieves, and troublemakers. They are often portrayed as taking pleasure in antagonizing cats and house-owners in particular. Sometimes they may be Karmic Tricksters , who retaliate in response to something else done to them first, but just as often they just like causing trouble. Resourceful Rodent may come into play and overlap with this if the mouse or mice are being clever and resourceful. Some mice even like to scare people because it's funny to them . Downplayed examples of this trope are mice that are mischievous, but have a good heart . Troublemaking or prankster mice can occupy a varied spectrum of morality depending on the situation and work in question. Some are Karmic Tricksters , playing mischief on blundering bullies and would-be bigshots, especially if these start making life difficult for the rodents first, and use their mockery and tricks to poke holes in their opponents' ego; this is especially prominent in media and narrative environments where Nice Mice is a prominent stereotype. In some countries, however, cultural depictions of mice lean more towards the negative side of this trope, and see them engage in more malicious acts of anarchy and in making trouble for no reason or for more selfish or wicked amusement. Still others are simply chaotic tricksters or scrappy thieves, stealing to get by or making trouble to amuse themselves without leaning strongly towards heroics or villainy. See also Hair-Raising Hare , Killer Rabbit , Screwball Squirrel , Suicidal Lemmings , Rascally Raccoon and You Dirty Rat! . Contrast Nice Mice .
